vix.note: VIX drops 8% to 16.2 as FOMC meeting begins; risk-on posture ahead of Wed Fed decision.

macro: FOMC meeting June 16-17 in focus: Fed widely expected to hold rates as core inflation moderates toward 2.7%. VIX plunges 8% to 16.2, signaling relief and risk-on positioning ahead of Wednesday's statement. Tech and semis rebounding sharply led by Intel (+2.6%), while gold and oil retreat from recent highs on dollar stability.
action: Monitor Wednesday FOMC statement for dovish language shift -- a hold with softer guidance could extend QQQ above $744 and trigger rotation into rate-sensitive names.

long.h: Google Doubles Down on Intel TPU Partnership
long.p: Alphabet has gone all-in on its deal with Intel to produce custom Tensor Processing Units, leveraging Intel foundry capacity to meet surging AI compute demand. The expanded partnership is a significant win for Intel's turnaround narrative and a sign Google is diversifying away from TSMC dependency.

long.h: Analyst Puts 80% Odds on Tesla-SpaceX Merger
long.p: One prominent analyst is calling an 80% probability that Tesla and SpaceX will merge following SpaceX's record-setting IPO at a $1.8 trillion valuation. Tesla and SpaceX already share AI chip projects and overlapping ambitions, but a deal would dramatically alter Tesla's risk profile.

long.h: Nvidia Sells $20B in Debt as Capital Markets Test Appetite
long.p: Nvidia is issuing at least $20 billion in bonds, a record corporate debt offering that tests market capacity alongside elevated US Treasury supply. While Nvidia's balance sheet is strong, the scale of the raise raises questions about whether corporate and sovereign debt are crowding each other out.

long.h: Italy Probes Apple Cloud Services Under EU Digital Markets Act
long.p: Italy's antitrust regulator has opened a formal investigation into Apple's compliance with EU Digital Markets Act interoperability rules for cloud services. This adds to Apple's growing regulatory burden in Europe and could result in fines or mandated changes to iCloud integrations.

long.h: Microsoft Turns to Amazon AWS for GitHub AI Capacity Overflow
long.p: GitHub has faced severe capacity and reliability issues following an AI-driven code-generation surge, prompting Microsoft to route workloads to Amazon Web Services. The move is a notable win for AWS and highlights the scale of AI infrastructure demand exceeding any single provider's buildout pace.

short.h: SpaceX Ditches Newswires, Moves to X for Investor Comms
short.p: SpaceX announced it will publish all earnings and major announcements on its website and Musk's X platform, abandoning traditional newswire services -- a bold move that may complicate institutional investor monitoring.
Intel closed at $127.86, outperforming the broader market with a +2.64% gain driven by positive sentiment around the expanded Google TPU partnership and continued AI chip demand tailwinds.
Alphabet closed at $369.35, rising 2.47% and outpacing the S&P 500 as the Google-Intel chip deal and resilient search ad revenue supported bullish sentiment.

short.h: Sundar Pichai Faces Student Walkout at Stanford Over Israel, ICE Ties
short.p: About 200 Stanford graduates walked out and others booed Google CEO Sundar Pichai during his commencement speech over Google's government contracts with Israel and ICE. The protest adds reputational risk to Alphabet's ESG narrative.

short.h: Signaloid Launches Compute Engine AMI on AWS Marketplace
short.p: British firm Signaloid released its distribution-extended compute hardware as an AWS Machine Image, enabling finance and AI workloads with claimed orders-of-magnitude performance gains on AWS infrastructure.

notes.AAPL: At $296, Apple navigating EU DMA interoperability probe (Italy) on cloud services. India manufacturing ramp and services revenue are long-term growth anchors.
notes.AMZN: Amazon at $246; AWS winning AI infrastructure overflow from Microsoft/GitHub. Ad and Prime momentum building. Beneficiary of multi-cloud AI buildout.
notes.META: Meta at $593, off $628 peak on profit-taking. AI-driven ad targeting and Llama adoption driving revenue upside; Reality Labs losses remain a drag.
notes.GOOGL: Alphabet at $369; doubling down on Intel foundry for TPU production to meet AI compute demand. Core search resilient; antitrust headwinds manageable near term.
notes.TSLA: Tesla at $411; SpaceX IPO at $1.8T valuation fueling 80%-odds merger speculation. EV demand recovery in progress; merger narrative adds significant volatility.
notes.NFLX: Netflix holding $80-86 range; ad-supported tier and live content strategy (NFL, WWE) sustaining subscriber growth with improving margin profile.
notes.DIS: Disney near $100; streaming segment approaching profitability, Parks resilient, ESPN bundle executing. Re-rating potential if streaming hits sustained profit.
notes.BRKB: Berkshire at $495 and trending higher; defensive positioning with record cash pile provides optionality for market dislocation buying. Low risk anchor.
notes.DIA: Dow ETF at $518; broad-market participation beyond tech improving. Financials and industrials contributing to gains alongside FOMC-hold expectations.
notes.SILVER: Silver retreating from $75+ to $69; industrial demand softening. Precious metals under pressure as Fed hold strengthens dollar. Watch FOMC tone.
notes.OIL: USO at $121, down from $140+; OPEC+ supply concerns and softer demand weighing on crude. Geopolitical premium fading. Dollar strength a headwind.
